rocm_libraries.name: rocm-libraries
rocm_libraries.description: Monorepo that consolidates multiple ROCm libraries (math, primitives, machine learning, communication) to streamline development, CI and integration. Many individual librarie’s sources now live here. Includes hipBLAS, hipFFT, hipSPARSE, rocBLAS, rocFFT, rocSPARSE, rocPRIM, rocsolver, tensile, and more. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

rocm_libraries.name: hipBLAS
rocm_libraries.description: BLAS marshalling library exposing a consistent interface to BLAS operations. Sits above backend libraries (rocBLAS or cuBLAS) and marshals inputs/results. Now integrated in rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:2]{index=2}

rocm_libraries.name: hipBLASLt
rocm_libraries.description: Flexible BLAS-style API for general matrix-matrix multiply (GEMM) with more flexible layouts and algorithms than classic BLAS. Deprecated standalone; integrated into rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:3]{index=3}

rocm_libraries.name: rocBLAS
rocm_libraries.description: ROCm’s Basic Linear Algebra Subprograms library optimized for AMD GPUs (BLAS). Implemented in HIP; used by many higher-level numerical routines. Deprecated standalone; sources now integrated in rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:4]{index=4}

rocm_libraries.name: hipFFT
rocm_libraries.description: Library for computing Fast Fourier Transforms (FFTs). Provides efficient Fourier transform routines for scientific and signal processing workloads on AMD hardware. Standalone repo is deprecated; now part of rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:5]{index=5}

rocm_libraries.name: hipRAND
rocm_libraries.description: Random number generation library providing various RNG primitives (uniform, normal distributions etc.) optimized for ROCm. Integrated under rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:6]{index=6}

rocm_libraries.name: hipSOLVER
rocm_libraries.description: Solver library implementing LAPACK-style linear algebra factorizations/solvers (QR, LU, Cholesky, eigen decomposition). Now part of rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:7]{index=7}

rocm_libraries.name: hipSPARSE
rocm_libraries.description: Sparse matrix algebra interface that marshals calls to backend sparse libraries (e.g., rocSPARSE). Suitable for sparse linear algebra workloads. Integrated into rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:8]{index=8}

rocm_libraries.name: hipSPARSELt
rocm_libraries.description: Lightweight library for sparse operations optimized for next-gen AMD architectures.

rocm_libraries.name: hipCUB
rocm_libraries.description: HIP port of the CUB library, providing CUDA-like primitives (scan, sort, reduce, etc.) for parallel algorithms.

rocm_libraries.name: hipTensor
rocm_libraries.description: Tensor utility and primitive library for multi-dimensional tensor operations under ROCm.

rocm_libraries.name: rocPRIM
rocm_libraries.description: Primitive parallel algorithms (scan, sort, reduction) for HIP/ROCm, analogous to primitives in CUDA.

rocm_libraries.name: rocThrust
rocm_libraries.description: STL-like parallel template algorithms, similar to Thrust for CUDA, optimized for ROCm.

rocm_libraries.name: rocWMMA
rocm_libraries.description: Matrix multiply-accumulate primitives optimized for specialized matrix operations on AMD hardware.

rocm_libraries.name: composable_kernel
rocm_libraries.description: Template library to write performance-critical kernels (e.g., GEMM, reductions) portable across AMD architectures.

rocm_libraries.name: AMDMIGraphX
rocm_libraries.description: Graph optimization engine focused on neural network inference acceleration. Part of ROCm ML stack.

rocm_libraries.name: MIOpen
rocm_libraries.description: High-performance machine learning primitives library (convolutions, activations, pooling) for deep learning frameworks. Deprecated standalone; moved to rocm-libraries. :contentReference[oaicite:17]{index=17}

rocm_libraries.name: RCCL
rocm_libraries.description: Communication library providing multi-GPU collectives (all-reduce, broadcast) similar to NVIDIA’s NCCL, optimized for ROCm. :contentReference[oaicite:18]{index=18}

rocm_libraries.name: rocSHMEM
rocm_libraries.description: Partitioned global address space (PGAS) communication library for symmetric access.

rocm_libraries.name: vllm
rocm_libraries.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for LLMs, supporting both CUDA and ROCm backends for running large language models on AMD GPUs.

rocm_libraries.name: sglang
rocm_libraries.description: sGLang is an open-source programming language and LLM serving framework enabling fast, scalable deployment of large language models with multi-backend support, including ROCm.

rocm_libraries.name: aiter
rocm_libraries.description: AITER (AI Tensor Engine for ROCm) - A centralized repository containing high-performance AI operators for accelerating various AI workloads on AMD GPUs. Includes kernels from Triton, CK, HIP, and ASM for MHA, MLA, MoE, quantization, and more.

rocm_libraries.name: triton
rocm_libraries.description: Triton is an open-source language and compiler for writing highly efficient custom deep learning primitives. It provides a Python-based programming model for creating GPU kernels with automatic optimizations.
